mechanism: Gestalt perceptual grouping affects spatial area estimation. Identical items coalesce into single coherent whole, increasing perceived area. This area estimate drives quantity judgment. Variety breaks cohesion, reducing area salience and thus quantity perception.

Information incongruity violates expectancy and increases involvement. Source characteristics (expertise, certainty) create congruent or incongruent pairings. Incongruity prompts deeper processing of message arguments through elaboration. Processing strength determines persuasion outcome.

System threat activates defensive motivation equally across confidence levels, but individuals differ in defense method selection. Low-confidence individuals prefer indirect (ambiguous, subtle) behavioral expressions. High-confidence individuals prefer direct (explicit, unambiguous) expressions. Consumption domain offers flexible vehicles for both.

Visual depiction orients attention toward dominant hand, facilitating automatic motor simulation of object interaction. Perceptual resources compete with simulation; occupying motor pathways blocks or redirects simulation. Amount of simulation affects purchase intention through embodied cognition.

Horizontal spatial location is mapped to temporal direction via reading/writing system. Past-left, future-right conceptualization enhances fluency for temporally-structured information when image placement matches temporal mapping. Fluency increases product evaluation. Processing fits learned cultural orientation.

Construal level differs between seller and buyer roles. Sellers adopt abstract (high-level) representations emphasizing desirability-related features. Buyers maintain concrete (low-level) representations including feasibility aspects. Differential weighting of feature types drives valuation discrepancy. Construal level mediates role effect on price.
